title: Hand-rolled MCP server invited a class of bugs the official @modelcontextprotocol/sdk would not have
content: Day 3 morning's first cut at examples/two-agents/mcp-server.mjs hand-rolled the JSON-RPC 2.0 framing, the initialize handshake, the tools/list / tools/call dispatch, the notification serialization, and the recvBuffer abstraction. Cursor BugBot reviewed PR #21 and surfaced 7 findings on commit 08f1a14, of which 5 were structural to the hand-rolled abstractions: (a) `truncated` flag silently lost when `recvBuffer.splice(0, length)` returned a NEW array stripped of the custom property; (b) unhandled `error` event on AmsConnection (EventEmitter throws when no listener is attached) crashed the host process; (c) recvBuffer not cleared on ams_join replacement, so stale frames from the prior conversation leaked into the next ams_recv; (d) failed ams_join left the dead AmsConnection bound to `connection`, masking the proper "not_joined" guard with a confusing "WebSocket is not open" error; (e) liveUrl ignored its parameter and read a closure variable instead. Three of these (a, c, d) were fixed by Cursor Agent commits b02cc7f / 111868b / 9ba93d3 before the operator intervened. The lesson the operator made explicit: "we have to manually fix bugs that are not an issue in the SDK." Re-implementing the MCP server on top of the official @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (McpServer + StdioServerTransport + zod input schemas) eliminates classes (a)-(c) and (e) by construction — the SDK's protocol layer handles framing, the request handler signature carries typed validated args (no closure leak), and notification dispatch goes through Protocol.notification rather than a hand-rolled stdout writer. Class (d) is application-level and remains the wrapper's responsibility, but is now a single try/catch around `ready()` rather than tangled state management.

title: Migrated examples/two-agents/mcp-server.mjs to @modelcontextprotocol/sdk v1.29 — McpServer + StdioServerTransport + zod schemas
content: Replaced the hand-rolled JSON-RPC stack with the official Anthropic MCP SDK. Tools registered via server.registerTool(name, {description, inputSchema}, handler) where inputSchema is a Zod raw shape (not JSON Schema). Notifications sent via server.server.notification({method, params}) — accesses the underlying Protocol layer for non-standard ams/* methods. Buffer logic encapsulated in a RecvBuffer class with explicit truncated flag (no array-property tricks). Result: 12/12 PASS in test-mcp-pair.mjs, 8/8 PASS in test-close-codes.mjs (worker logic untouched by the SDK migration), demo PASS — all against local wrangler dev. Confidence: established. Alternatives considered: (i) keep hand-rolled and add tests for each found bug — rejected as inferior to using a maintained SDK that prevents the bug class entirely; (ii) Cloudflare's `agents/mcp` McpAgent — rejected because that's the Worker-hosted /mcp scenario (next slice), not the local stdio scenario this PR ships. Reversibility: two-way — the wire protocol (AMS) is unchanged, so a future hand-rolled server or a different SDK stays interoperable. Disconfirmer: if the SDK introduces a regression that the hand-rolled version did not have, revisit.

title: Worker handleConnect wrapped in try/catch → wsClose(4500, "internal_error") to match DO defense-in-depth (last open BugBot finding on PR #21)
content: BugBot finding 3c0402b0 on PR #21 commit 9ba93d3 (the only finding the bot's own follow-up commits did not address): the comment in handleConnect promised "Pre-upgrade HTTP errors only fire when the request isn't even a WS upgrade attempt," but resolveConnect could throw on KV failure, JSON.parse of a corrupt KV record, or a crypto error in pepperedHash, AND stub.fetch could throw — all of which would propagate as HTTP 500 instead of WS close 4500. Fixed in worker/src/index.ts:118-145 by wrapping the resolveConnect + stub.fetch chain in try/catch with a `wsClose(4500, "internal_error")` fallback. Mirrors the DO's existing protection at conversation.ts:39-51. Verified by tsc clean and the existing test-close-codes.mjs (the success path is unchanged; the catch only fires on unexpected throws which the test deliberately doesn't trigger).

title: Cursor Agent self-fixed 6 of the 7 BugBot findings on PR #21 between 08f1a14 and 9ba93d3
content: Cursor Agent commits b02cc7f (preserve recvBuffer truncated flag + dedupe wsClose helper), 111868b (clear recvBuffer on join/leave + listenerCount guard around AmsConnection error emit), and 9ba93d3 (reset connection on failed ams_join + use liveUrl parameter) addressed BugBot bug IDs a7c5fce3, 78297ba5, 0e55f71c, ref1_6af1e4aa, b4131976, and 5c2053e3 respectively. The seventh finding (3c0402b0 — handleConnect lacks try-catch) was left for human/operator follow-up and is fixed in this commit. The bot's autofixes are correct and surgical; the SDK migration in this same commit makes most of those fixes redundant by construction (the underlying classes the bugs lived in are gone). Both cleanups are kept in the history as the documented chain of evidence — bot patch → SDK migration that subsumes it.

title: The hosted /mcp endpoint (SessionDO) — when it lands — uses Cloudflare `agents/mcp` McpAgent, NOT a port of this stdio server
content: mcp-server.mjs is the local-stdio reference for SPEC §3.1 items 4+5. The hosted wrapper at /mcp on the Worker is a different runtime (Durable Object behind Streamable HTTP) with different lifecycle, different transport, and different auth-binding posture. When the hosted slice ships, it should use Cloudflare's `agents/mcp` McpAgent (the Worker-side equivalent of @modelcontextprotocol/sdk's stdio server) rather than wrapping this stdio server in a Worker. The two are sister implementations sharing the AMS wire and the same six-tool surface; they are not the same code. Documented in examples/two-agents/README.md "What's not in scope" section.

title: PR #21 has the SDK migration + the last BugBot fix; tests still local-only — fresh validator runs them against the live deploy
content: The third commit on this branch (after the bot's three) brings: (1) Worker handleConnect try-catch → 4500 (BugBot finding 3c0402b0); (2) examples/two-agents/mcp-server.mjs rewritten on @modelcontextprotocol/sdk v1.29 (McpServer + StdioServerTransport + zod schemas) — 12/12 PASS in test-mcp-pair.mjs against local wrangler dev; (3) test-mcp-pair.mjs sends notifications/initialized after initialize per the MCP handshake spec the SDK enforces; (4) examples/two-agents/README.md updated to reference the SDK + name `agents/mcp` McpAgent as the future-hosted shape; (5) examples/two-agents/package.json adds @modelcontextprotocol/sdk@^1.29.0 alongside ws@^8. The fresh-session validator checklist from the prior journal still stands: live-deploy re-run of all three example scripts against AMS_HOST=https://ams.klappy.dev AND AMS_HOST=https://ams.truthkit.ai, then tag v0.1.0 from the merged commit on ACCEPT.
